indicators: Four distinct emission lines are present.
The colors of the lines are violet, blue, cyan, and red.
The violet and blue lines are closely spaced on the far left (shorter wavelengths) of the spectrum.
A cyan line is observed to the right of the violet and blue lines.
A distinct red line is observed on the far right (longer wavelength) of the spectrum, with significant spacing from the other lines.
This pattern of four lines with their specific colors and relative positions (violet, blue, cyan, red) corresponds precisely to the Balmer series of hydrogen.
